---

title: "Synthetic_controls"
author: "annon for peer review"
date: "2025-11-22"
output: html_document

---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}

library(readxl)
library(dplyr)

data<- read_excel("data_filtered.xlsx", 
    sheet = "data_filtered")


eu_real <- c(
  "Austria", "Belgium", "Bulgaria", "Croatia", "Cyprus (European Union)",
  "Czech Republic", "Denmark", "Estonia", "Finland", "France",
  "Germany", "Greece", "Hungary",  "Italy",
  "Latvia", "Lithuania", "Luxembourg", "Malta", "Netherlands",
  "Poland", "Portugal", "Romania", "Slovakia", "Slovenia",
  "Spain", "Sweden"
)

data <- data %>%
  
  filter(Year <= 2020)

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(proxy)
library(cluster)

# STEP 1: Filter pre-period
pre <- data %>% 
  filter(Year <= 2020) %>%
  select(Domicile_named_country, Year, Applicants)

# STEP 2: Build country x year wide matrix
traj <- pre %>%
  group_by(Domicile_named_country, Year) %>%
  summarise(Applicants = sum(Applicants), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Year, values_from = Applicants) %>%
  as.data.frame()

rownames(traj) <- traj$Domicile_named_country
traj <- traj[ , -1] # drop name column

# STEP 3: log + scale (normalize shape)
traj_scaled <- scale(log(traj + 1))

# STEP 4: compute Euclidean distance between all countries
dist_mat <- as.matrix(dist(traj_scaled, method = "euclidean"))

# isolate treated (EU) vs donor (non-EU)
treated <- rownames(traj_scaled) %in% eu_real
donors <- !treated

dist_treated_donors <- dist_mat[treated, donors]

# STEP 5: nearest neighbors per EU country
find_matches <- function(row) {
  donor_names <- colnames(dist_treated_donors)
  donor_names[order(row)][1:3]
}

matches <- apply(dist_treated_donors, 1, find_matches)

matches<-as.data.frame(matches)
```

```{r}
writexl::write_xlsx(matches, "eu_country_matches_long.xlsx")
```
